title: BRD-3 — Non-Functional Requirements
text:
<content>
BRD-3 — NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS (v1.0)
Carry-forward (extended for v1.0)
- NFR-ARCH-1 (carry, permanent) — No backend, ever. DD-117. v1.0 trust ledger and team aggregate are file-only. /coherence:trust sync writes only local + committed files. No network egress at any point.
- NFR-ARCH-2 (carry, permanent) — No legacy version support. DD-118. v1.0 installs fresh — no migrator from v0.4. .claude/coherence/ per-project state preserved across re-install; personal trust-ledger.json survives.
- NFR-PRIVACY-N5 (extended) — Telemetry consent unchanged. Trust ledger never crosses developer boundaries: personal ledger gitignored; only the developer's own author-hash file is written to coherence/trust/ via explicit sync.
- NFR-COMPAT-N5 (carry) — parseMajor correctness from v0.4 carries; v1.0.x is the same major bucket per the SemVer major-digit rule.
- NFR-COST-1 (carry) — Per-session cost ≤ v0.1 baseline × 1.30. --deep LLM call is user-triggered and bounded by 10-pair cap (\~\$0.05 worst case). Free-tier audit is non-LLM. Trust ladder is non-LLM.
New for v1.0
- NFR-TRUST-1 (new) — Trust ledger atomic writes via write-temp-then-rename pattern (consistent with v0.3 state writes). Concurrent-write test in M-LEDGER-1.
- NFR-TRUST-2 (new) — Personal trust ledger capped at 200 events per sectionRef (LRU eviction). Bounds file size and recomputation cost. Older events evicted at write time.
- NFR-PRIVACY-N6 (new) — Team aggregate files (coherence/trust/\<author-hash\>.json) contain only the hashed author identity. No clear-text email, no IP address, no machine identifier, no commit hash, no path metadata.
- NFR-PERF-N6 (new) — /coherence:metrics renders \< 200 ms p95 with 90-day metrics.jsonl + 1000-section trust ledger + 20 active team developer files.
- NFR-PERF-N7 (new) — /coherence:audit free tier \< 100 ms p95. --deep cost gate (estimate + confirmation prompt) \< 50 ms (before LLM call). Hash check for section-symbol-index.json cache validity \< 50 ms for 1000-section index.
- NFR-PERF-N8 (new) — Trust score recomputation O(min(n, 200)) per section. Stop-hook latency contribution \< 20 ms p95 even with 100 sections affected per session.
- NFR-SECURITY-N2 (new) — Local release fails closed on missing signing capability. --unsigned flag required for explicit unsigned release with -UNSIGNED.tgz suffix. Prevents silent shipment of unsigned artifacts when developer runs release without CI.
- NFR-OBS-2 (new) — Trust ledger writes emit telemetry events (proposal_accept_recorded, proposal_revert_recorded) per the v0.3 DD-068 redaction matrix. No clear-text section content. No author email.
——— BRD-3 AUDIT AMENDMENTS 2026-05-13 ———
- NFR-PATH-SANDBOX (extended, DD-128 carry) — The --out path sandboxing from v0.4 DD-128 applies to all v1.0 commands that accept --out: /coherence:metrics --out, /coherence:export-metrics --out (carry from v0.4). Paths outside projectRoot refused unless --allow-out-of-tree flag passed (stderr warning). Promoted from implicit reference (FR-METRICS-2) to explicit NFR for clarity.
- NFR-OBS-2 REVISED — Original wording declared event names (proposal_accept_recorded, proposal_revert_recorded). Event declarations are functional and moved to FR-TELEMETRY-1. NFR-OBS-2 now scoped to: telemetry compliance — all trust ledger events follow v0.3 DD-068 redaction matrix. No clear-text section content. No author email. No machine identifier. Audit log entries (coherence-log.md) gain ISO-timestamped lines for trust promotion events.
——— BRD-3 AUDIT PASS 3 AMENDMENTS 2026-05-13 ———
- NFR-PERF-N6 amendment (pass 3) — /coherence:trust --status performance bound. /coherence:trust --status renders in \< 200 ms p95 with the same workload assumption as /coherence:metrics (90-day metrics.jsonl + 1000-section trust-ledger + 20 active team developer files). Justification: both commands compute the team aggregate per section (FR-LEDGER-4 “no aggregate cache”). Same algorithm, same data, same bound.
</content>
